name: HTTP API Server
description: JSON HTTP API on configured port (default 7700). Provides endpoints for access graph registration, blast radius computation, trust score queries, taint reset, authority mapping, canary injection/results, full feedback reports, and OTLP findings ingestion. Uses Flask as the HTTP framework (justified exception to stdlib preference: manual routing on http.server would exceed 300 lines and introduce bugs in request parsing, content negotiation, and error handling). All responses are JSON with Content-Type: application/json. Error responses use a machine-readable ErrorResponse envelope with StrEnum error codes. The HTTP layer is strictly a translation layer — zero business logic — delegating all computation to engine Protocol interfaces injected via a ServerContext. Three-module structure: server.py (app factory, routes, startup/shutdown), models.py (Pydantic v2 request/response models), errors.py (ErrorCode enum, global error handlers, ValidationError translation).

types.name: NodeId
types.description: Type-safe string identifier for a node in the access graph. NewType over str.

types.name: RunId
types.description: Type-safe string identifier for a canary or report run. NewType over str.

types.name: ReviewId
types.description: Type-safe string identifier for a review that authorizes taint reset. NewType over str.

types.name: TrustScore
types.description: Float in [0.0, 1.0] representing raw computed trust. Annotated[float, Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)].

types.name: ErrorCode
types.description: Machine-readable error codes returned in all error responses. StrEnum.

types.name: TrustTier
types.description: Display tier derived from raw trust score. Used only for display, never for policy calculations. StrEnum.

types.name: DataClassificationTier
types.description: Classification tier for data sensitivity. StrEnum.

types.name: FindingSeverity
types.description: Severity level for findings reported via OTLP adapter. StrEnum.

types.name: ErrorResponse

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code from ErrorCode enum.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message including the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Optional additional structured error context (e.g., field names, validation errors).

types.description: Standard error envelope for all HTTP error responses.

types.name: ServerConfig

fields.name: host
fields.description: Bind address for the HTTP server.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port number for the HTTP server.

fields.name: debug
fields.description: Enable Flask debug mode. Must be false in production.

fields.name: max_request_size_bytes
fields.description: Maximum request body size in bytes (default 10MB).

validators.error_message: Max request size must be between 1KB and 100MB.

types.description: Configuration for the HTTP API server. Loaded from YAML config file.

types.name: ServerContext

fields.name: config
fields.description: Server configuration.

fields.name: trust_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed trust engine instance for score queries and taint reset.

fields.name: blast_radius_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed blast radius computation engine.

fields.name: authority_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed authority map engine.

fields.name: canary_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed canary injection and results engine.

fields.name: report_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed report generation engine.

fields.name: findings_engine
fields.description: Protocol-typed OTLP findings ingestion engine.

types.description: Dependency injection container holding references to all engine Protocol instances. Passed to the Flask app factory to wire routes to engines. Composition over inheritance.

types.name: AccessGraphPayload

fields.name: nodes

fields.name: edges

types.description: Request body for POST /register — an access graph in JSON form.

types.description: A single node in the access graph.

fields.name: access_type

fields.name: node_count

functions.name: create_app
functions.description: Flask app factory. Creates and configures the Flask application, registers all route blueprints, wires engine dependencies from the ServerContext, and registers global error handlers. Returns a configured Flask app ready to serve.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Dependency injection container with all engine instances and server configuration.

error_cases.name: missing_engine
error_cases.condition: One or more engine references in context is None

error_data.detail: All engine Protocol instances must be provided in ServerContext

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: ServerConfig fails Pydantic validation

preconditions: context.config is a valid ServerConfig with port in [1, 65535]
All engine references in context are non-None and implement their respective Protocol interfaces
No Flask app has been created from this context yet (single app per context)
postconditions: Returned Flask app has all 10 routes registered (9 endpoints + /health)
Global error handlers are registered for ValidationError, domain exceptions, and unexpected exceptions
Content-Type defaults to application/json for all responses
App is not yet running (caller must invoke run_server or app.run)

structured_side_effects.description: Logs app creation, route registration, and engine wiring

functions.name: run_server
functions.description: Start the HTTP server on the configured host and port. Blocks the calling thread. Uses Flask's built-in threaded server (appropriate for sidecar use case, not production internet-facing).

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Server context with configuration and engine instances.

error_cases.name: port_in_use
error_cases.condition: The configured port is already bound by another process

error_cases.name: startup_timeout
error_cases.condition: Server fails to bind within 3 seconds

preconditions: context.config.port is available and not already bound
All engines are initialized and ready
postconditions: Server was listening on context.config.host:context.config.port until shutdown
All open connections are closed on return

structured_side_effects.description: Binds and listens on the configured TCP port
Logs server start, ready state, and shutdown

functions.name: handle_register
functions.description: POST /register — Ingest an access_graph.json payload. Validates the AccessGraphPayload, delegates to the blast_radius_engine for graph storage, returns node/edge counts and registration timestamp.

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: Request body is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: graph_invalid
error_cases.condition: Edges reference node_ids not present in nodes list, or duplicate node_ids found

error_cases.name: engine_failure
error_cases.condition: Blast radius engine raises an unexpected exception during graph storage

preconditions: request_body has been deserialized and validated as AccessGraphPayload
All node_ids referenced in edges exist in the nodes list
No duplicate node_ids in the nodes list
postconditions: Access graph is stored and available for blast radius queries
RegisterResponse.node_count == len(request_body.nodes)
RegisterResponse.edge_count == len(request_body.edges)
RegisterResponse.registered_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Stores the access graph for subsequent blast radius queries
Logs registration with node/edge counts

functions.name: handle_blast_radius
functions.description: POST /blast-radius — Compute the blast radius from a given node. Validates the BlastRadiusRequest, delegates to blast_radius_engine for BFS/DFS traversal, returns affected nodes with depth and classification data.

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: Request body is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: request_body.node_id is not in the registered access graph

error_cases.name: computation_failed
error_cases.condition: Blast radius engine raises an error during traversal

preconditions: An access graph has been registered via POST /register
request_body.node_id exists in the registered access graph
postconditions: BlastRadiusResponse.origin_node_id == request_body.node_id
All affected nodes are reachable from origin within max_depth hops
BlastRadiusResponse.total_affected == len(BlastRadiusResponse.affected_nodes)
BlastRadiusResponse.max_depth_reached <= request_body.max_depth
BlastRadiusResponse.computed_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query against the access graph; no mutation

functions.name: handle_get_trust
functions.description: GET /trust/<node_id> — Retrieve the current trust score, display tier, taint status, and last 20 ledger events for a node. Delegates to trust_engine.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node identifier to query trust for.

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id is not known to the trust engine

error_cases.name: trust_computation_failed
error_cases.condition: Trust engine raises an error during score computation

preconditions: node_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: TrustResponse.node_id == node_id
TrustResponse.trust_score is in [0.0, 1.0]
TrustResponse.recent_events has at most 20 entries, ordered most recent first
TrustResponse.trust_tier is derived from trust_score for display only
TrustResponse.queried_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query against the trust engine and ledger; no mutation

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) for score, O(k) for last k events where k=20

functions.name: handle_reset_taint
functions.description: POST /trust/reset-taint — Clear taint lock on a node with a review_id. Appends a taint_reset event to the ledger (never mutates existing entries). Delegates to trust_engine.

inputs.name: request_body
inputs.description: Taint reset parameters including node_id, review_id, and reason.

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: Request body is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: request_body.node_id is not known to the trust engine

error_cases.name: review_not_found
error_cases.condition: request_body.review_id does not correspond to a valid review

error_cases.name: taint_reset_failed
error_cases.condition: Trust engine fails during taint reset (e.g., node not tainted)

error_cases.name: ledger_write_failure
error_cases.condition: Ledger append fails due to I/O or checksum error

preconditions: request_body.node_id exists in the trust engine
request_body.node_id currently has a taint lock
request_body.review_id is a valid, non-empty review identifier
request_body.reason is between 1 and 2000 characters
postconditions: A new taint_reset event has been appended to the trust ledger (append-only, no mutations)
The node's taint lock is cleared
TaintResetResponse.ledger_event_id is the ID of the newly appended event
TaintResetResponse.new_trust_score reflects the score after taint reset
TaintResetResponse.reset_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Clears the taint lock on the specified node
Appends a taint_reset event to the JSONL trust ledger
Logs taint reset with node_id, review_id, and reason

functions.name: handle_get_authority
functions.description: GET /authority — Retrieve the full authority map. Authority is declared from manifests, distinct from trust (which is computed from the ledger). Delegates to authority_engine.

error_cases.name: authority_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Authority engine is not initialized or manifests not loaded

postconditions: AuthorityResponse.total_entries == len(AuthorityResponse.entries)
AuthorityResponse.queried_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
Authority entries reflect declared authority from manifests, not computed trust

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query against the authority engine; no mutation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of authority entries

functions.name: handle_canary_inject
functions.description: POST /canary/inject — Seed a canary corpus into the system. Canary fingerprints are structurally valid for their tier, globally unique per run, and impossible in real data (UUIDs in domain-shaped strings). Delegates to canary_engine.

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: Request body is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: injection_failed
error_cases.condition: Canary engine fails during injection

preconditions: request_body.run_id is unique and has not been used before
request_body.target_tiers is non-empty
request_body.canary_count is between 1 and 1000
postconditions: CanaryInjectResponse.run_id == request_body.run_id
CanaryInjectResponse.injected_count == request_body.canary_count * len(request_body.target_tiers)
All injected canaries are recognizable as synthetic (never plausible real data)
All injected canaries contain UUIDs in domain-shaped strings
CanaryInjectResponse.injected_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Seeds canary corpus into the canary tracking system
Logs canary injection with run_id, tier count, canary count

functions.name: handle_canary_results
functions.description: GET /canary/results/<run_id> — Retrieve the taint escape report for a canary run. Shows which canaries escaped their expected tier. Delegates to canary_engine.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The canary run identifier to query results for.

error_cases.name: run_not_found
error_cases.condition: run_id does not correspond to a known canary injection run

error_cases.name: results_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Canary engine fails to compute or retrieve results

preconditions: run_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: CanaryResultsResponse.run_id == run_id
CanaryResultsResponse.total_escaped == len(CanaryResultsResponse.escapes)
CanaryResultsResponse.queried_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query against canary engine; no mutation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of escape events

functions.name: handle_get_report
functions.description: GET /report/<run_id> — Retrieve the full feedback report for a given run. Includes trust summary, findings summary, canary summary, and blast radius summary. Delegates to report_engine.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: The run identifier to generate/retrieve the report for.

error_cases.name: run_not_found
error_cases.condition: run_id does not correspond to any known run

error_cases.name: report_not_found
error_cases.condition: Report generation fails or report data is unavailable

preconditions: run_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: ReportResponse.run_id == run_id
ReportResponse.generated_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
Report reflects the state of all engines at query time

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query against report engine; no mutation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the total data points in the report

functions.name: handle_ingest_findings
functions.description: POST /findings — Receive OTLP span JSON from the Baton adapter. The adapter layer is ground truth; node self-reports are claims. Validates findings, delegates to findings_engine for processing and ledger updates.

inputs.name: request_body
inputs.description: Findings payload from the OTLP adapter.

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: Request body is not valid JSON

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic model validation

error_cases.name: ingestion_failed
error_cases.condition: Findings engine raises an error during ingestion

preconditions: request_body.findings is non-empty
request_body.source is a non-empty string identifying the adapter
postconditions: FindingsResponse.accepted_count + FindingsResponse.rejected_count == len(request_body.findings)
Accepted findings have been persisted and may trigger trust score updates
FindingsResponse.ingested_at is a valid ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

structured_side_effects.description: Persists accepted findings and may trigger trust score recalculations
May append trust-relevant events to the JSONL trust ledger
Logs findings ingestion with source, accepted/rejected counts

functions.name: handle_health
functions.description: GET /health — Server health and readiness check. Returns status, version, uptime, and engine readiness. Used for cold start verification (target: ready in under 3 seconds).
postconditions: HealthResponse.status is 'ok' or 'degraded'
HealthResponse.uptime_seconds >= 0
HealthResponse.engines_ready reflects actual engine initialization state

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only health check; no mutation

functions.name: handle_error
functions.description: Global error handler registered on the Flask app. Catches ValidationError (from Pydantic), known domain exceptions (NodeNotFound, RunNotFound, etc.), and unexpected exceptions. Transforms all exceptions into ErrorResponse JSON with appropriate HTTP status code, machine-readable error_code, and human-readable message including the specific entity that caused the error.
postconditions: Returned ErrorResponse has a valid ErrorCode
ErrorResponse.message includes the specific node_id, field, run_id, or domain that caused the error
Malformed JSON bodies produce error_code INVALID_JSON with HTTP 400, not HTTP 500
Unexpected exceptions produce error_code INTERNAL_ERROR with HTTP 500
ValidationError produces error_code VALIDATION_ERROR with HTTP 400 and field-level details

structured_side_effects.description: Logs the error with full exception context, stack trace for 500s

invariants: All HTTP responses have Content-Type: application/json
All error responses conform to the ErrorResponse schema with a machine-readable error_code from ErrorCode enum
Zero business logic in the HTTP layer — all computation delegated to engine Protocol interfaces
No endpoint exposes ledger mutation (edit/delete); only append operations via reset-taint
All datetime fields are UTC with ISO 8601 serialization
Error messages always include the specific node_id, field, run_id, or domain that caused the error
Trust and authority response types are never shared or conflated
All policy calculations use raw trust score (float), never display tiers for computation
The trust ledger is append-only — reset-taint appends a reset event, never mutates existing entries
Server binds to configured port (default 7700) and serves synchronously with threading
